channel.title: Pages on WhaleDolphin's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on WhaleDolphin's Blog

item.title: About
item.description:
Hello, stranger. My name is Whale Dolphin (鲸鲵), a name derived from “The Rhapsody of the Goddess of the Luo River,” which reads “Whales and salamanders surge alongside the wheel hubs, while water birds soar as escorts.” In this context, whale and salamander refer to male and female aquatic mammals respectively. I’ve actually given them lovely individual names: Whale Fall (鲸落) and Salamander Whisper (鲵笙). Consequently, I’ve named their habitats “Whale Fall’s Pond” and “Salamander Whisper’s Ocean.” My English name is simply Whale Dolphin! I use this name across all platforms, so if you come across any of these names, it’s most likely me.

This blog is written for myself, but if you happen to stumble upon my blog posts and are interested enough to click on this page, perhaps we share a special connection. 🥹

As mentioned, I’m a two-dimensional otaku programmer who loves singing and is fond of vsingers and vtubers. My favorite singer is Luo Tianyi; her voice gives me strength. Currently, my research focuses on speech synthesis, and I hope to develop a CV-level synthesis model. My dream is to create AI digital characters as vivid as those in the two-dimensional world.

If you share similar thoughts, feel free to use the contact information on the left to email me or add me as a friend. 😘

P.S. Some Chinese traditional poem and expression about ACG is translated by Claude3.5-Sonnet.

item.title: Links
item.description:
To use this feature, add links section to frontmatter.

This page’s frontmatter:

links:
 - title: GitHub
 description: GitHub is the world's largest software development platform.
 website: https://github.com
 image: https://github.githubassets.com/images/modules/logos_page/GitHub-Mark.png
 - title: TypeScript
 description: TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript.
 website: https://www.typescriptlang.org
 image: ts-logo-128.jpg

image field accepts both local and external images.
